title: Shady RAT: A Decade of Digital Hoarding
hook: A hidden worm fed on global secrets for ten years, stealing data from governments, banks and tech firms without a single hand seen.

evidence_links.label: Navigating the nexus: geopolitical, international relations and technical dimensions of US-China cyber strategic competition (Nistha Kumari Singh, Amrita Jash, 2025)
Regulating Cyber Space: An Examination of U.S.-China Relations (Adam K. Ghazi‐Tehrani, 2016)
Coordinating uncertainty in the political economy of cyber threat intelligence (Rebecca Slayton, Lilly Pijnenburg Muller, 2025)

connections.transition_line: Just as the ghost that stole secrets in Shady RAT, GhostNet’s silent fingers slipped through the same veins of the global network.

layers.content:
Security firms finally shut down the central server, but the worm’s code was released publicly.

The release sparked debates: while it brought the tool out, it also enabled new attacks.

The true origin remains a blank spot in history, reminding us that hidden digital weapons can run for a decade before they are seen.

The world grapples with a ghost that still sits in the cloud, unseen but ever present.
